tools.name: create_anim_blueprint
tools.description:
Create an Animation Blueprint (ABP_) asset targeting a skeleton.

An Animation Blueprint has TWO isolated execution realms:
  1. EventGraph: Standard K2 Blueprint logic — cache Pawn velocity, booleans, rotation from the
     owning character. THIS is what inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d populates.
     CRITICAL: NEVER directly read gameplay state in the AnimGraph — always cache via EventGraph
     to prevent thread-safety crashes.
  2. AnimGraph: Blend nodes, state machines, skeletal controls (IK). This MUST be authored in
     the editor UI — it uses specialized nodes not available in T3D.

Workflow:
1. create_anim_blueprint — create the ABP targeting the character's skeleton
2. inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d (graph_name='EventGraph') — add variable caching logic
3. assign_anim_blueprint — assign the ABP to the SkeletalMeshComponent of the character BP
4. (In editor) open the AnimGraph to create state machine and blend nodes

Naming convention: ABP_ prefix (e.g. ABP_HeroCharacter, ABP_Enemy)

Use get_anim_info to find the skeleton path before creating the AnimBP.

asset_path.description: Output content path for the Animation Blueprint, e.g. /Game/Animations/ABP_HeroChar

skeleton_path.description: Content path of the target USkeleton asset, e.g. /Game/Characters/SK_Hero_Skeleton. Use search_assets to find it if unsure.

parent_class.description: Parent C++ class for the AnimBP. Default: AnimInstance. Custom parent classes must derive from UAnimInstance.

tools.name: import_animation_fbx
tools.description:
Import an FBX animation file and create a UAnimSequence asset targeting a specific skeleton.

Requirements:
  - The FBX file must exist on the local disk (absolute path)
  - The skeleton asset must already exist in the project
  - Bone names in the FBX must match the target skeleton hierarchy

After import:
  - Use create_anim_montage to wrap sequences into playable montages
  - Use assign_anim_blueprint to drive animation via an AnimBP
  - Use get_anim_info to list all sequences compatible with a skeleton

source_file.description: Absolute path to the FBX file on disk, e.g. C:/Users/user/Desktop/Run.fbx

skeleton_path.description: Content path of the target USkeleton asset, e.g. /Game/Characters/SK_Hero_Skeleton

destination_path.description: Content folder for the created AnimSequence. Default: /Game/Animations

asset_name.description: Name for the created AnimSequence asset, e.g. AS_Run. If omitted, uses the FBX filename.

tools.name: assign_anim_blueprint
tools.description:
Assign an Animation Blueprint to a SkeletalMeshComponent inside an existing Blueprint.

This sets the AnimClass on the component template, which drives the character's animations at runtime.

IMPORTANT: Use the _C generated class path (append _C to the ABP asset path):
  - ABP asset path: /Game/Animations/ABP_HeroChar
  - Generated class path: /Game/Animations/ABP_HeroChar.ABP_HeroChar_C

Use get_blueprint_info to confirm the component name (typically 'Mesh' for Character parent class).

asset_path.description: Content path of the Blueprint to modify (the character Blueprint)

component_name.description: Variable name of the SkeletalMeshComponent in the Blueprint SCS. Typically 'Mesh' for ACharacter subclasses. Use get_blueprint_info to confirm.

anim_blueprint_path.description: Content path of the Animation Blueprint GENERATED CLASS (_C suffix), e.g. /Game/Animations/ABP_HeroChar.ABP_HeroChar_C

tools.name: create_anim_montage
tools.description:
Create an AnimMontage asset from one or more AnimSequences on a skeleton.

AnimMontages are used for gameplay-triggered animations (attacks, interactions, death) via PlayMontage in Blueprint.

After creation:
  - Open in the editor to add slots, sections, and notify events if needed
  - Wire to Blueprint via inject_blueprint_nodes_t3d using PlayMontage (MemberParent UAnimInstance or USkeletalMeshComponent)

Naming convention: AM_ prefix (e.g. AM_Attack_Light, AM_Death)

Use get_anim_info to get the list of available sequences for the target skeleton.

asset_path.description: Content path for the AnimMontage, e.g. /Game/Animations/AM_Attack

skeleton_path.description: Content path of the USkeleton asset

sequences.description: Content paths of AnimSequence assets to include in the montage (the first sequence is the source)

tools.name: get_anim_info
tools.description:
Read-only query: return animation asset information for a skeleton or Animation Blueprint.

Returns:
  - skeleton_name and skeleton_path
  - compatible_sequences: all AnimSequences in /Game that target this skeleton
  - compatible_montages: all AnimMontages targeting this skeleton

Call this BEFORE create_anim_blueprint or create_anim_montage to find existing assets and avoid duplicates.

Provide either a Skeleton asset path or an AnimBlueprint asset path as asset_path.

asset_path.description: Content path of a USkeleton or UAnimBlueprint asset to query
